step3_wmma_64x64_grid.interpretation: multi-warp kernel; each warp writes disjoint output tile so no inter-warp bar.sync needed

step4_wmma_cp_async.interpretation: cp.async.commit_group/wait_group pairs (2 each) for double-buffer prefetch

The retry2 'hexa 2.0x win' from earlier in this session was DEFINITIVELY bogus (3-arg launch with garbage k_tiles + wrong B layout + wrong cuBLAS arg-order). §10 closure row 'HGEMM ≥ 50% cuBLAS' remains MET at M=N=K=256.

honest_scope: All measurements are codegen-side oracles + small-budget GPU silicon fires ($0 - $0.01 per round).

HGEMM 5-trial reproduces PR #214's 0.500x — retracted 'hexa 2.0x win' (retry2) was caused by INVALID launch params, NOT a cuBLAS or hexa regression. Per @D g3 honest, 0.500x stands as the canonical M=N=K=256 ratio.
ULP and NaN/Inf checks validate codegen-emitted IEEE arithmetic semantics. They do NOT claim any §10 closure flip (which is perf-oriented).
PTX-text safety audit is a CODEGEN-DENSITY check, NOT a runtime safety proof. setp+bra count and bar.sync count are necessary-but-not-sufficient for safety.
Nsight Compute installed but profile run deferred. Future cycle could use ncu data to find specific codegen optimization targets (register pressure, occupancy, memory throughput per kernel).
